_doc: The neutral-noun → per-platform-concrete map. The core (PROTOCOL.md, the roles, the checklists) names every act with a neutral noun; this one table resolves each to its platform tool. A reader who needs the concrete consults this; the prose stays neutral. Single home — never copy this mapping elsewhere. Adding a platform = add a column.

the-enforcement-layer.opencode: tool.execute.before plugin (throw, deny) + chat.message plugin (push part, inject)

class_support._doc: Which enforcement return classes each platform realises. A rule whose class is unsupported falls back to persona (recorded per-rule in deny-rules.json `fallback`). Claude realises all three (deny + ask via PreToolUse, inject via UserPromptSubmit). OpenCode realises deny (tool.execute.before, throw) and inject (chat.message — a per-turn part pushed before the LLM call, the analog of UserPromptSubmit); ask falls back to persona (no ask-return in the plugin hooks). Both OpenCode classes are live-verified on opencode 1.17.x: deny blocked a write into .ai-pm/tooling/, and inject fired on an unconfigured project — the no-config-run-setup nudge reached the model and the orchestrator offered setup.

models._doc: Per-platform model policy for the project config's role `model` field (ai-pm.config.json). ZERO-CONFIG by default — works with no setup. `session` = the orchestrator's model. `auto` = a different model for independent blind spots IF the platform offers one out of the box, ELSE it falls back to `session` (never an error). Names are environment-dependent — that is why this lives in the adapter, not the neutral config. This block is also the home of the **list-available-models** contract point (architecture.md `## Extension points`): per platform, HOW setup learns which models the environment offers — `discover` below.

claude.auto: the allow-listed model opposite the session's (opus<->sonnet) — always available, so cross-model review works zero-config
claude.discover: the known fixed pair above (`allow`) — no enumeration call; the model space on Claude IS this pair, so the allowlist is meaningful and gates a pin.

opencode.discover: run-a-shell-command `opencode models` and parse the `provider/model` ids it prints (one per line). If the command is absent or errors (older opencode, sandbox), fall back to a guided dialog where the Operator types/confirms the id — never invent one.
opencode._note: ZERO-CONFIG ⇒ `auto`/`session`/absent ⇒ one session model everywhere: same-model review, no cross-model independence (a pin equal to the session model gives none either — state that honestly to the Operator). On OpenCode the ENVIRONMENT is the authority, not a static list: what `opencode models` reports (or the pin names) is what is usable, so `allow: []` is NOT a hard gate — it is an explicit 'no baked-in environment ids in the shared core' marker (env ids live only in a project's own ai-pm.config.json). To OPT IN to cross-model review, set `roles.reviewer.model` to a per-platform pin `{ "opencode": "provider/model" }` in the project's own config; the install step bakes it into the assembled reviewer (adapter/opencode/install-agents.mjs). A pin is honoured as-is.
